impact_evidence: Environmental | Air & Climate | Dust generation -> "As a global mining giant Anglo American had significant means at its disposal to remedy the spread of dangerous lead dust and consequent blood contamination at life threatening levels after it received the reports it had commissioned." || Environmental | Land, Soil & Geology | Topsoil degradation and loss of soil fertility -> "for example by removing and replacing contaminated soil in the area" || Social | Health, Safety & Well-being | Community exposure to dust, noise, pollution -> "Kabwe, in Zambia, was for 49 years the site of one of the world's most productive lead mines ... | Accidents and fatalities (including structural failures such as tailings dams) -> "The first records of child fatalities from lead poisoning were during the period in which Kabwe was an Anglo human rights, as well as the UN Working Groups on Business and Human Rights and Discrimination against Women and Girls, have argued that by opposing the class action Anglo American is acting contrary to the United Nations Guiding Principles on Business and Human Rights, to which Anglo American is a signatory." || Governance | Regulation, Compliance & Institutions | Enforcement of environmental and social regulations -> "Anglo American failed to ensure that recommendations by scientific experts it had commissioned to address the risk, for example by removing and replacing contaminated soil in the area, were implemented." | "This profitable lead mine was within the Anglo American Group for almost 50 years ... and the company failed to facilitate any clean-up process upon leaving, despite environmental damage -> "failure of Anglo American to clean up" | "Insofar as Anglo American has shown no moral responsibility towards the impacts of its historic pollution, the only realistic and management between companies and communities -> "An open letter signed by 25 civil society organisations was handed to its London HQ in 2024 by a representative from Kabwe, asking Anglo American to governance -> "It is clear that the Kabwe mine site was not remediated upon closure of the mine in 1994, some 20 years post nationalisation, despite ZCCM acknowledging responsibility for all historic liabilities relating to Kabwe mine following nationalisation."

title: Africa's big copper countries set their sights on the profits of trade | MarketScreener
impact_evidence: Governance | Reputation & Social License | Mining image and public perception -> "Investors' perception of Congo and Zambia, which straddle the African Copperbelt, is that they are difficult places to invest, hence Verisk Maplecroft's Resource Nationalism Index categorises them as high risk." || Social | Equity, Rights & Vulnerable Groups | Wealth distribution and inequality -> "As a means to reward resource-holding governments, dividends have generated friction as governments have questioned whether the amounts paid to them are fair." || Social | Livelihoods & Economy | Business opportunities (local supply chains, services) -> "Zambia and Mercuria in December set up a jointly owned copper trading unit that has started negotiations with almost all the producers in the country, two separate sources told Reuters." | "Mercuria has set aside an initial budget of about $500 million to buy copper from local producers, backed by additional lines of credit as more metal supply becomes available, one of the sources said." || Social | Livelihoods & Economy | Income generation and poverty reduction or exacerbation -> "Zambia plans to start by buying copper on commercial terms, before negotiating for physical metal equal to its shareholding, instead of just relying on dividend payouts for profits as it has until now, the two sources said." || Governance | Stakeholder Engagement & Rights | Stakeholder inclusion in decision-making -> ""One can foresee disputes around the division of mineral production and who trades with whom as readily as those that have previously emerged around dividend payments," he said."
